title: The Lanterne of Light
summary: The checked-in XML is a pinned repository copy of University of Michigan CME item AHA2749. U-M encoded selected runs of Lilian M. Swinburn's 1917 EETS edition: title page, contents, introduction, the complete edited text on pp. 1-137, appendix, and notes, but not the glossary on pp. 153-192. Swinburn transcribed the complete treatise from British Library Harley MS 2324, ff. 1r-128v; Harley MS 6613 is a distinct, imperfect collateral witness. A reported parchment copy made for John Claydon was condemned in 1415 and does not survive. A circa-1535 Robert Redman print survives as a later transmission state and has a separate EEBO-TCP encoding. James M. Dean's 1996 edition supplies a later partial scholarly path based on an electrostatic print of Harley 2324 and checked against Swinburn. Cached deliverables preserve the pinned CME XML and license, the complete public Swinburn scan, official catalog metadata, the complete TCP text, the complete Dean anthology carrier, pinned METS repository evidence, and rights and reproduction-service evidence; no current public manuscript facsimile was found for either surviving Harley witness.
review_notes: The anonymous medieval work is distinct from every manuscript, print, edition, scan, encoding, and repository artifact represented here.
Harley MS 2324 is Swinburn's complete base witness. Harley MS 6613 is collateral and is not represented as a source of Swinburn's text.
The exact textual relationships among the two surviving manuscripts, the Redman print, and Claydon's reported copy require collation and are not inferred from shared work identity.
John Wycliffe is an excluded historical attribution: the work's dependence on the 1409 Constitutions postdates his death in 1384.
John Grime copied Claydon's lost book; Robert Redman printed the later edition. Neither activity establishes medieval authorship.
The Folger record describes one imperfect physical copy of STC 15225, but the exact physical exemplar behind EEBO image set 8704 remains unresolved.
Dean's published selection locator and his acknowledgments' electrostatic-print folio ranges conflict; the intermediary's exact coverage is therefore left unresolved.
The Dean edition is currently labeled Legacy HTML. Its pinned official repository contains only a reduced PDF, README, and LICENSE, so no exact public Dean XML deliverable is asserted.
Public-domain determinations are jurisdiction-specific. Modern photography, editorial contributions, provider interfaces, and contractual terms remain separate.
British Library catalog text is CC BY 4.0; that license does not cover the manuscripts or future reproduction photographs.
The CME submodule's LICENSE expressly speaks of software, while the root README discusses code and output; neither clearly resolves reuse of this inherited XML data artifact.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for aha2749

Three DIV1 and eighteen DIV2 elements retain Swinburn's editorial frame, thirteen-chapter text, appendix, and notes.

entities.label: University of Michigan CME item AHA2749
entities.description: The public electronic edition represented by the repository XML. It encodes Swinburn's title page, contents, introduction, complete text, appendix, and notes, but omits the glossary.

entities.label: Lilian M. Swinburn's edition of The Lanterne of Liȝt (1917)
entities.description: Complete EETS scholarly edition based on Harley MS 2324, with introduction, edited text, appendix, notes, and glossary.

bibliographic.citation: The Lanterne of Liȝt, ed. Lilian M. Swinburn, Early English Text Society, Original Series 151 (London: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trübner & Co. for EETS, 1917).

bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society, Original Series 151

entities.label: Internet Archive Vanderbilt/Google scan of Swinburn (1917)
entities.description: Complete 230-page provider object preserving the full printed edition, including the glossary omitted by AHA2749.
entities.reproduction_format: Image-container PDF with item metadata and scandata
notes: Internet Archive metadata flags degraded PDF generation because of invalid JP2 headers, but qpdf reports no PDF syntax or stream-encoding errors in the preserved direct download.

entities.label: British Library, Harley MS 2324
entities.description: The complete first-quarter-fifteenth-century parchment witness used throughout Swinburn's edition.

date_statements.label: Production
date_statements.value: First quarter of the fifteenth century

notes: The work occupies ff. 1r-128v.
Small red initials; Latin quotations underlined in red according to Swinburn; post-1600 binding.
Swinburn milestones use recto/verso as a/b, from 1a through 128b.
No current public whole-codex image service was found.

entities.label: British Library catalog record for Harley MS 2324
entities.description: Official Archives and Manuscripts catalog description and JSON representation.

entities.label: British Library, Harley MS 6613
entities.description: The other surviving manuscript copy, imperfect through lost leaves within the text and at the end.

holding.notes: The catalog requires a letter of introduction for consultation.

physical_description.extent: 57 folios plus flyleaves; a stub remains between ff. 7 and 8
notes: The imperfect work occupies ff. 1r-57v.
Blue initials with red flourishes, red rubrics, blue paraphs, and occasional contemporary marginalia.
It is a collateral witness, not a demonstrated source of Swinburn's 1917 edition.
No current public whole-codex image service was found.

entities.label: British Library catalog record for Harley MS 6613
entities.description: Official Archives and Manuscripts catalog description and JSON representation.

entities.label: Reported copy of The Lanterne of Light owned by John Claydon
entities.description: A red-leather parchment book in a good English hand, copied for John Claydon by John Grime and used as evidence at Claydon's 1415 heresy trial.

holding.notes: Historical ownership only; no present holding exists.

notes: Trial evidence described a good English hand.
Its exact contents and readings cannot be recovered from the surviving reports.
Claydon was burned in September 1415, and the condemned book is not among the two manuscript copies identified as surviving by the British Library; its precise loss history is not established here.
No descent relation to Harley 2324, Harley 6613, or the Redman print is asserted.

entities.label: Robert Redman's circa-1535 print of Here begynnethe the lanterne of lyght
entities.description: A later early-print transmission state, modeled with the nearest available entity type; it is not a modern critical edition and is not asserted to descend directly from either surviving Harley witness.

bibliographic.citation: Here begynnethe the lanterne of lyght ([London]: Robert Redman, [1535?]), STC 15225; ESTC S108268.
bibliographic.title: Here begynnethe the lanterne of lyght

notes: The Folger record carries historical associations with John Grime and William Hardy; these are not adopted as confirmed authorship of the medieval work.
The Folger copy lacks E8 and I10 including the colophon; I9 is torn; photostats supply missing matter from another copy.

entities.label: Folger catalog record for STC 15225
entities.description: Official bibliographic and copy-specific record for the Folger's imperfect Harmsworth copy.

entities.label: Early English Books microfilm 103:06
entities.description: Microfilm surrogate associated with STC 15225 in the TCP source description; the physical copy filmed has not been independently identified.

entities.label: EEBO page-image set 8704
entities.description: Digital page-image set cited by TCP as the image source for A05083; page images are separate from TCP's CC0 keyboarded text.
entities.reproduction_format: Subscription page images derived from microfilm

entities.label: EEBO-TCP transcription A05083
entities.description: Complete keyboarded and encoded transcription of the circa-1535 Redman print, created in TCP Phase 2 and publicly released under CC0.
notes: The print declares thirteen chapters in its table. The TCP structural division count does not independently encode every declared chapter as a separate DIV and should not be used as a chapter census.

entities.label: British Library electrostatic print of Harley MS 2324 used by James M. Dean
entities.description: A research reproduction intermediary used to edit Dean's selection. Published locators and the acknowledgments' stated supplied folios conflict, so exact coverage is unresolved.
notes: Dean's selection locator gives Harley 2324 ff. 1v-4r and 5r-20v, while the acknowledgments report British Library electrostatic prints of ff. 1r-4r and 5v-13r.
No public copy of this working reproduction was found.

entities.label: James M. Dean's The Lanterne of Light: Prologue and Chapters 3-5 (1996)
entities.description: Partial scholarly edition within Medieval English Political Writings, based on an electrostatic print of Harley MS 2324 and checked against Swinburn.

bibliographic.citation: James M. Dean, ed., 'The Lanterne of Light: Prologue and Chapters 3-5,' in Medieval English Political Writings (Kalamazoo: Medieval Institute Publications, 1996), 75-91.
bibliographic.title: The Lanterne of Light: Prologue and Chapters 3-5

entities.label: METS digital edition of Dean's Lanterne selection
entities.description: Public legacy-HTML web representation of the 1996 selection and introduction. No exact public Dean XML deliverable was located in the edition interface or pinned official repository.

entities.label: METS downloadable PDF of Medieval English Political Writings
entities.description: Complete 291-page image-only anthology carrier; the Lanterne selection occupies PDF pages 96-112, corresponding to printed pp. 75-91.
notes: qpdf validates the preserved provider file with recoverable linearization-hint warnings; the byte-identical source download is retained without repair.

entities.label: METS GitHub reduced PDF of Medieval English Political Writings
entities.description: Complete 291-page image-only anthology carrier in the official dean-political repository. It preserves the same edition content as the provider-object PDF but is a byte-distinct 19,650,697-byte digital carrier; the Lanterne selection remains at PDF pages 96-112, corresponding to printed pp. 75-91.
notes: The exact pinned remote file has SHA-256 cee4e2e09add467d2fbc72a12becca938bff626ad7032179d19d58162ed4311b and is not cached locally because the larger complete provider-object PDF already preserves the same anthology content.
A transient qpdf check succeeded with recoverable linearization/shared-object hint warnings; the remote PDF was not retained in the local cache.

access.access_method: British Library Archives and Manuscripts catalog.
Request the shelfmark through the British Library Reading Rooms item-request form; registration and eligibility rules apply.

First ask whether an existing complete digital surrogate of Harley MS 2324 is available. If none exists, request a quote for expert full-color photography of the entire physical codex: covers and boards, spine, pastedowns, flyleaves and endleaves, blanks, stubs, and every recto and verso folio or other surface. Separately identify ff. 1r-128v as the target-work portion. Only if the complete codex is infeasible should an exact-folio or partial order be considered.
access.contact: British Library digitisation order form; licensing@bl.uk for intended publication or reuse questions
access.format: Expert photography; exact deliverables and quote subject to provider assessment
notes: The service says precious or rare manuscripts require expert photography rather than ordinary scanning.
The price list supplies per-image rates but does not establish the final number of photographs, handling category, image format, delivery terms, or total quote for this manuscript.
This request strategy does not imply service approval, feasibility, a total price, or permission to reuse or publish delivered images; obtain those terms in writing separately.

rights.component: Checked-in inherited XML encoding and markup
rights.jurisdiction: Repository license context and intended place of reuse
rights.basis: The pinned CME LICENSE expressly addresses software, while the root README addresses code and generated output; neither clearly licenses inherited source XML data or editorial markup.
notes: The medieval underlying text and the particular repository encoding are separate rights layers.

rights.component: Materials presented in U-M CME item AHA2749
rights.basis: U-M's current item result states that the materials are in the public domain and applies No Copyright-United States.
notes: The assertion is U.S.-specific and does not resolve the submodule's independent data-license wording.

rights.component: 1917 printed edition, including Swinburn's editorial contribution
rights.basis: The edition was published in 1917, before the current U.S. public-domain cutoff of 1 January 1931 stated in Circular 15A.
notes: No worldwide or current United Kingdom determination is asserted.

rights.component: Modern scan files and provider metadata
rights.jurisdiction: Provider terms and intended place of reuse
rights.basis: The underlying 1917 volume is public domain in the United States, but no item-specific rights or license field was found for the scan reproduction.

rights.component: Physical-material ownership, custody, and access conditions for British Library Harley MS 2324
rights.basis: This record addresses custody and access to the physical manuscript only. Rights in the underlying medieval text are a distinct layer. This physical-object record does not license photographs, facsimiles, images, or reproductions.

rights.component: British Library catalog text and JSON metadata

notes: The catalog license does not cover manuscript images.

rights.jurisdiction: Historical physical-property, custody, survival, and access context
rights.basis: This record addresses the reported physical manuscript's historical custody and loss only; no modern repository or surviving object is known. Rights in the underlying medieval text are a distinct layer. This physical-object record grants no license for any image, facsimile, or reproduction that may later be identified.

rights.component: Circa-1535 printed text and typography
rights.basis: The print was published about 1535, centuries before the current U.S. public-domain cutoff.

rights.component: Folger catalog metadata and copy notes

rights.component: Keyboarded and encoded TCP text

notes: The dedication excludes EEBO page images and supplementary files.

rights.component: British Library manuscript reproduction supplied to Dean
rights.jurisdiction: Provider terms and intended place of reuse
rights.basis: The reproduction is documented only as an electrostatic print supplied for research; no public license or surviving copy was verified.

rights.component: Dean's 1996 edited selection, introduction, notes, and other original editorial contribution
rights.basis: The modern edition was published in 1996 and METS restricts digital editions to individual use, directing reprint requests to its Executive Director.
notes: This does not place the anonymous medieval underlying text under copyright.

rights.component: Legacy-HTML digital representation, interface, and modern editorial presentation
rights.jurisdiction: Provider terms and intended place of reuse
rights.basis: METS limits digital editions to individual use and directs reprint requests to its Executive Director. No exact Dean XML deliverable was located, so the site's separate license statement for XML is not applied to this legacy-HTML representation.
notes: The site-wide statement that all XML is CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 would govern an identified XML file, but it neither proves that Dean XML exists nor licenses the HTML interface, PDF, or other assets.

rights.component: METS downloadable anthology PDF and modern edition content
rights.jurisdiction: Provider terms and intended place of reuse
rights.basis: METS limits digital editions to individual use and requires contact for reprinting digital text. This record applies to the cached 39,298,503-byte provider-object PDF.
notes: The official GitHub repository's 19,650,697-byte reduced PDF is byte-distinct and is covered by a separate CC0 rights record; that repository license is not transferred to this cached provider-object PDF.

rights.component: Dean-Political-reduced.pdf in the official METS Editions repository at commit be31152ae3714b55b2aa1267dc350230c0b24216
rights.basis: The pinned official repository contains the reduced PDF, a README describing the PDF and license, and a CC0 1.0 Universal LICENSE. This records the provider's CC0 dedication for that exact repository deliverable.
notes: CC0 operates only to the extent of the affirmer's rights and is not generalized here to the byte-distinct cached provider-object PDF or unrelated provider assets.

rights.component: U.S. Copyright Office Circular 15A
rights.basis: The circular is a work of the United States Government, for which copyright protection is unavailable under 17 U.S.C. § 105.

rights.jurisdiction: Provider terms and intended place of reuse

editorial_practices.description: Swinburn expanded manuscript contractions and represented supplied letters in italics. Italics in the source edition therefore often preserve editorial expansion boundaries rather than manuscript letterforms.
notes: A manuscript facsimile is still needed to inspect the exact abbreviation signs corresponding to each expansion.

editorial_practices.description: Swinburn states that capitalization was modernized rather than diplomatically retained.

editorial_practices.representation: Generally retained, with editorial changes where useful
editorial_practices.description: Swinburn generally retained manuscript punctuation but changed it when she judged alteration useful.

editorial_practices.representation: Title page, contents, introduction, text, appendix, and notes; glossary omitted
editorial_practices.description: U-M encoded the complete medieval text and substantial editorial matter but omitted Swinburn's glossary on pp. 153-192.
Dean presents a bounded partial edition for a political-writings anthology, not a complete edition of the treatise.

editorial_practices.source_feature: British Library electrostatic print of Harley MS 2324
editorial_practices.representation: Manuscript-based partial text checked against Swinburn
editorial_practices.description: Dean states that the selection was based on an excellent electrostatic print of the Harley manuscript and checked against Swinburn's edition.

evidence.summary: Lists £27.70 for the first photographic TIFF, £7.25 for subsequent images, and £58.19 per image for bespoke complex handling, while leaving manuscript-specific totals to a quote.

evidence.citation: Folger Shakespeare Library, Here begynnethe the lanterne of lyght, STC 15225, catalog record 162738.
evidence.summary: Confirms the Redman imprint, conjectural date, extent, STC and ESTC identifiers, historical attributions, Folger shelfmark, and copy-specific missing and damaged leaves.

evidence.citation: Text Creation Partnership, A05083.xml, commit 1a00312ce7056dea66e7b3f4045fc28636b42991.
evidence.locator: TEI header, source description, table of contents, and complete BODY
evidence.summary: Identifies STC 15225, ESTC S108268, EEBO citation 99843937, image set 8704, microfilm 103:06, April 2011 Phase 2 production, and the complete encoded Redman text.

evidence.citation: Text Creation Partnership, A05083 TEI availability statement and repository license notice.
evidence.summary: Dedicates TCP's keyboarded and encoded text to the public domain under CC0 1.0 while excluding page images and supplementary files.

evidence.citation: James M. Dean, ed., 'The Lanterne of Light: Prologue and Chapters 3-5,' in Medieval English Political Writings (1996).
evidence.summary: Supports anonymous authorship, probable 1409-1415 date, selection boundaries, Harley folio locators, and Dean's statement that his text was based on an electrostatic print and checked against Swinburn; the current edition landing page identifies the provider format as Legacy HTML.

evidence.citation: Medieval English Political Writings, ed. James M. Dean (1996), complete METS downloadable PDF.
evidence.locator: Acknowledgments p. xvi / PDF page 11; selection pp. 75-91 / PDF pages 96-112
evidence.summary: Preserves the complete anthology carrier, verifies the target selection's PDF range, and records the acknowledgments' electrostatic-print folios that conflict with the selection locator.

evidence.citation: METS Editions, dean-political repository recursive tree at commit be31152ae3714b55b2aa1267dc350230c0b24216.
evidence.summary: The exact pinned tree contains only Dean-Political-reduced.pdf (19,650,697 bytes; git blob 9db25afd3adc1ec1b44bca7ff6ea71009871d25e), LICENSE (7,048 bytes), and README.md (238 bytes). It contains no XML or TEI file. The reduced PDF's transiently verified SHA-256 is cee4e2e09add467d2fbc72a12becca938bff626ad7032179d19d58162ed4311b, distinct from the cached provider-object PDF.

evidence.citation: METS Editions, dean-political README.md at commit be31152ae3714b55b2aa1267dc350230c0b24216.
evidence.summary: States that the repository contains a PDF of the entire edition and a license for use; it names no XML or TEI deliverable.

evidence.citation: METS Editions, dean-political LICENSE at commit be31152ae3714b55b2aa1267dc350230c0b24216.
evidence.summary: Contains the CC0 1.0 Universal legal code associated with the pinned repository and its distinct reduced PDF.

evidence.citation: METS Editions, Copyright and Citation.
evidence.summary: Limits digital editions to individual use, directs reprint requests to the Executive Director, and states that all XML is CC BY-NC-SA 4.0. This site-wide policy does not by itself prove that an XML deliverable exists for Dean's legacy-HTML edition.

evidence.citation: U.S. Copyright Office, Circular 15A, Duration of Copyright, rev. April 2026.
evidence.summary: States the current U.S. duration framework, including public-domain status from 1 January 2026 for works published before 1 January 1931.

evidence.citation: 17 U.S.C. § 105, Subject matter of copyright: United States Government works.
evidence.summary: Provides that copyright protection under Title 17 is not available for works of the United States Government.

evidence.citation: Kyvero-Press/CME LICENSE at pinned commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c.
evidence.summary: Contains an Unlicense-style grant expressly framed around software, leaving its intended scope for inherited source data and XML editorial markup unclear.

evidence.citation: General Corpus README.org license section.
evidence.summary: States GPL-3.0 for code and CC BY-SA 4.0 for output except public-domain works, but does not clearly characterize or license inherited source XML data.

open_questions.question: Can complete full-color digital facsimiles of Harley MSS 2324 and 6613 be obtained or made publicly accessible?
next_steps: First ask the British Library whether an existing complete digital surrogate, including any legacy or unpublished surrogate, exists for either shelfmark.

If no complete surrogate exists, request the whole physical codex in color, including covers and boards, spine, pastedowns, flyleaves and endleaves, blanks, stubs, and every recto and verso folio or other surface, rather than only the work portion.
Associate the target work with Harley 2324 ff. 1r-128v and Harley 6613 ff. 1r-57v inside the complete requested objects.
Use an exact-folio or partial order only as a fallback if a complete-codex surrogate is infeasible.
Ask for written reuse and publication terms before placing any delivered photographs in a public repository.
notes: Legacy FullDisplay and Viewer URLs now redirect to a generic British Library digitised-collections page rather than exposing item images.

open_questions.question: What are the reading-level relationships among Harley MSS 2324 and 6613, the Redman print, and the reported Claydon copy?
next_steps: Collate both surviving manuscripts and the complete Redman state.
Consult recent stemmatic and Lollard transmission scholarship.
Do not add direct descent edges without explicit evidence.

open_questions.question: Which physical copy was filmed for EEBO microfilm 103:06 and image set 8704?
next_steps: Inspect the EEBO record through a subscribing institution for source-library and shelfmark metadata.
Compare image imperfections against the Folger copy before linking the image set to that physical copy.

open_questions.question: What folios were actually present in the British Library electrostatic print used by Dean?
next_steps: Ask James M. Dean or Medieval Institute Publications whether the editorial files survive.
Reconcile the selection locator ff. 1v-4r, 5r-20v with the acknowledgments' ff. 1r-4r, 5v-13r before asserting exact reproduction coverage.

open_questions.question: Will METS Editions publish an exact XML or TEI file for Dean's currently legacy-HTML edition?
next_steps: Monitor the official dean-political repository for an XML or TEI addition.
Ask METS Editions whether an unpublished XML conversion exists or is planned.
If an exact XML file appears, cache the pinned provider bytes and apply the provider's XML license only to that identified deliverable.

open_questions.question: What license governs redistribution and commercial reuse of the inherited AHA2749 XML encoding and its editorial markup?
next_steps: Ask Kyvero Press and U-M whether the data file is intended to fall under the CME software license or U-M's No Copyright-United States assertion.
Document any response separately from rights in the medieval text and the 1917 edition.
Do not infer that the root repository's output license automatically licenses inherited source data.

open_questions.question: What is the current non-U.S. copyright status of Swinburn's 1917 editorial contribution and what provider terms would govern redistribution of each scan or reproduction?
next_steps: Determine Swinburn's death date and apply the law of each intended jurisdiction.
Obtain item-specific permission or terms for modern manuscript photographs, Internet Archive derivatives, and METS files before redistribution.
